title: Five patients recover from Ebola in DR Congo and leave hospital

originalTitle: UK wins court case over collapsed Rwanda asylum deal
title: UK wins court case over collapsed Rwanda asylum deal
content: A court has ruled the UK does not need to pay Rwanda after the UK pulled a deal between the two countries.

originalTitle: Nigeria police warn against reprisal attacks against South Africans
title: Let's Be Kind Across Africa!
content:
Hello, Junior Reporters of Africa!

Welcome to your special news report! Today, we’re going to talk about something very important: being kind and friendly to everyone, no matter where they come from. It’s like being a good friend to all your classmates, even if they come from different parts of your town or a different school.

Big Feelings in South Africa

Sometimes, grown-ups can have big, upset feelings. This happened recently in a country called South Africa. Imagine some people getting really, really upset because others who weren’t born in South Africa were living and working there. These upset feelings started to grow, making some people sad and scared. Did you know? South Africa is a beautiful country with amazing wildlife, like lions and elephants! It's also famous for its delicious 'braai' (barbecue) food.

What Are These Upset Feelings About?

When people were very upset, they sometimes stood together in groups and shouted about what was bothering them. This is called a 'protest'. They were protesting against 'migrants'. A migrant is just a person who moves from their home country to live in another country. Think of it like moving to a new house in a new town, but a much bigger move across countries! Many people from other African countries, like Nigeria, live and work in South Africa. They move there to find good jobs, or to go to school, or to be safe. Just like your family might move to a new place for a better life, people move between countries for good reasons too.

Nigeria Says 'Stop the Anger!'

When news came to Nigeria that some people from Nigeria were being treated badly in South Africa, many people in Nigeria became very sad and angry. They felt sad for their brothers and sisters in South Africa. Some people in Nigeria started to get angry at South Africans who live in Nigeria. They thought, 'If they are hurting our people, maybe we should hurt their people!' This is called a 'reprisal attack'. It means hurting someone because someone from their country hurt someone from your country. But the police in Nigeria quickly said, "STOP! This is not the right way!" The police are there to keep everyone safe, no matter where they come from. They said it is very, very important not to hurt innocent people. It's like if two kids from different schools had a fight, and then all the kids from one school tried to hurt all the kids from the other school. That would be very unfair and wrong, wouldn't it?

Why is Being Friends So Important for Africa?

Africa is a huge continent with many, many countries – 54 countries, to be exact! It's like a really, really big family. Imagine if all the members of a big family were always fighting. It wouldn't be a happy family, would it? Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has a rich history and many different cultures, languages, and traditions. We all live on the same continent, under the same African sky. It's super important for all African countries and all African people to be friends and help each other. When we are kind and respectful, our continent becomes stronger and happier for everyone.

Learning and Sharing Together

Sometimes, people might be scared of things they don't understand, or people who are different from them. But being different is a wonderful thing! It means we have more to learn and more to share. Did you know? Nigeria has over 250 different groups of people, each with their own language and traditions! South Africa has 11 official languages! How cool is that? Instead of being angry or scared, we can try to understand why people move. We can learn about their cultures and share our own. We can remember that everyone wants to feel safe, loved, and have a good life.

What Can You Do?

Even though these big problems happen, you, our junior reporters, can make a difference! You can be a peace hero! 1. **Be kind to everyone:** No matter where your friends or neighbours come from, be kind to them. 2. **Learn new things:** Ask questions about different countries and cultures. It's fun to learn! 3. **Share your toys and smiles:** A simple smile can make someone's day better. 4. **Talk it out:** If you have a problem, try to talk about it calmly, just like the leaders in Nigeria and South Africa are trying to talk to each other to find solutions. The police in Nigeria are working hard to make sure everyone is safe. Leaders from Nigeria and South Africa are talking together to solve these problems and make sure everyone can live peacefully. They are working to heal the upset feelings. So let's remember the big lesson from today: Choose kindness, choose friendship, and let’s all help make Africa a happy and peaceful home for everyone! Keep shining, junior reporters!
summary: Police in Nigeria have told people not to hurt South Africans living in Nigeria, after some Nigerians were treated badly in South Africa, reminding everyone that we should always be kind and friendly to people from all countries.
whyMatters: It's important to be kind and friendly to everyone, no matter where they come from, so that all people in Africa can live together in peace and safety, like a big, happy family.

originalTitle: Kuwait condemns Iranian attack as Iran-US trade new strikes
title: Friends Want to Be Safe! News from Far Away
content:
Hello, my super friends! It's your friendly reporter here, bringing you news from far, far away. Sometimes, even grown-up countries have little disagreements, just like we sometimes argue with our friends. But the most important thing for everyone, everywhere, is to be safe and happy! Let's learn a little bit about what happened across the big blue ocean.

News from Far Away

Imagine a land called Kuwait. It's a country very far from us, across the big blue ocean! One day, the people in Kuwait heard some strange noises high up in the sky. It made them a little worried, just like when you hear a strange sound outside your window at night and wonder what it is. So, what did they do? They made sure their special 'sky shield' was working! Did you know? Kuwait is a country in the Middle East, a part of the world that has lots of sand and sunshine! It's a bit like a big, warm desert, with a beautiful blue sea next to it. People there live in cities and have special food and stories.

What is a Sky Shield?

A 'sky shield' is a very clever thing called 'air defence'. Imagine you have a special, invisible umbrella that can catch anything bad coming from the sky, like a big, strong magnet! That's what air defence is for countries. It helps keep them safe from things that might fly over them that shouldn't be there. Kuwait turned on its sky shield to be extra safe and to watch the sky carefully. Did you know? In some parts of Africa, we have very tall mountains that act like natural shields, protecting villages from strong winds! Nature has its own wonderful ways to keep us safe, just like clever grown-ups make special shields.

Countries That Talk Big

There are other countries involved in this story too, called Iran and the United States, or the US. These are also very big countries, even further away than Kuwait! Sometimes, the grown-ups who are in charge of these countries, like presidents or leaders, have big discussions or disagreements. It's like when you and your friend have different ideas about which game to play, but much, much bigger, and with whole countries! Did you know? The world has almost 200 countries! Each one has its own special flag, like our different school uniforms, but for a whole country! And each country has its own language, songs, and tasty foods.

When Grown-Ups Don't Agree

The news said that Kuwait called something that happened 'not nice' or 'heinous'. This means they thought something very bad happened, and they were sad and upset about it. They want everyone in their country to be safe and friendly. Just like when someone says something mean to you, and you tell them that's not okay, countries also tell each other when something is not okay. Did you know? Talking is always the best way to solve problems. When we talk nicely and listen to each other, we can understand each other better and find a happy solution. For grown-up countries, this special talking is called 'diplomacy'!

What is a 'Strike'?

The news also talked about 'strikes'. This doesn't mean hitting a ball like in football or when workers don't go to work! In grown-up news, sometimes 'strikes' means a country sends something to another country, like a very strong message. It can mean a quick action to show they are upset. It's like when two friends are very upset and keep arguing back and forth, trying to show who is right, but with bigger actions. However, grown-ups always try to solve these problems without hurting anyone, and they want to make peace. Did you know? Many African countries work together in groups, like the African Union, to help each other and solve problems peacefully. It's like a giant club of friends who promise to help each other and make sure everyone is safe and happy.

Leaders Talk for Peace

When things like this happen, important grown-ups called 'leaders' talk to each other. They talk on the phone, or they meet in special places to discuss. They try very hard to make sure everyone is safe and that problems are fixed without anyone getting hurt. Their most important job is to look after the people in their country and make sure they can play, learn, and live without worry. They want peace, just like we all do. Did you know? Many leaders around the world have won special awards, like the Nobel Peace Prize, for working very hard to bring people together and stop fighting. Peace is a very important goal for everyone!

Why We Learn About It

It's good for us to know a little bit about what's happening in the world, even in places very far away from us in Africa. It helps us understand that everyone, no matter where they live, wants to be safe, just like us. And it reminds us that being kind and friendly to everyone, even if they are different, is always the best way to live. We can all be peace heroes in our own lives! So, let's all remember to be kind and friendly to our neighbours, our school friends, and everyone around us. Because when we are kind, we help make the whole world a safer and happier place for everyone, everywhere! High five for peace!
summary: Countries far away, like Kuwait, used a special 'sky shield' to stay safe after hearing strange noises, as grown-ups in charge of different countries like Iran and the US sometimes have big disagreements, but they are all trying to find peaceful ways to stay safe.
whyMatters: It's important to know that countries, just like friends, sometimes have big disagreements, but grown-up leaders work hard to talk and keep everyone safe and peaceful, so we can all live happily without worry.

title: French navy, backed by the UK, intercepts Russian oil tanker
content: According to France, the tanker, which had sailed from Murmansk, was trying to 'skirt international sanctions'.

originalTitle: Israel PM orders strikes on Beirut suburbs as Hezbollah conflict escalates
title: Big News for Little Ears: What's Happening in Faraway Lands
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids!

Hello, my wonderful friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you important stories from around the world. Sometimes, the news can sound a bit big and grown-up, but don't worry! I'm here to help us understand it all together, in a way that makes sense to our bright young minds. Today, we're going to talk about something happening very far away from our beautiful homes here in Africa. It's about two countries that are neighbours, just like you might have a friend living next door.

What is Happening?

There are two countries called Israel and Lebanon. They are very, very far from us, in a place called the Middle East. Sometimes, grown-ups in different countries have big disagreements, and sadly, they might fight. That is what is happening here. Recently, the leader of Israel, a grown-up called Mr. Benjamin Netanyahu, said something important. He said that his country would hit certain places in a city in Lebanon. This city part is called Dahieh. He said this because some people in Israel were hurt by attacks from a group that lives in Lebanon, called Hezbollah. So, it's like a big argument that is getting more serious.

Who are Israel and Lebanon?

Imagine our continent, Africa, with all its wonderful countries like Kenya, Nigeria, South Africa, and Ghana. Well, Israel and Lebanon are two countries in another part of the world. They are next-door neighbours! People live in these countries, just like we live in ours. They have homes, schools, parks, and shops. Kids play there, just like you do. **Did you know?** Countries usually have their own special flags and sometimes speak different languages! It's amazing how many different places and people there are in the world.

Who is Mr. Benjamin Netanyahu?

Every country has a leader, right? Someone who helps make big decisions for everyone living there. For example, here in Africa, we have presidents or prime ministers who lead our countries. Mr. Benjamin Netanyahu is the Prime Minister of Israel. He is like the main captain of a big team, and his team is the country of Israel. He is in charge of keeping his country and its people safe. When he speaks, it means he is making important choices for his country. These choices can affect many, many people.

What is Hezbollah?

Now, let's talk about Hezbollah. This is a group of people who live in Lebanon. They have very strong beliefs about how their country should be and sometimes they fight to protect what they believe in. Think of it like a very big club or a very big team. But this team sometimes uses fighting instead of talking to solve problems with others. They are based in Lebanon and sometimes have disagreements with Israel. It's important to remember that not all people in Lebanon are part of Hezbollah, just like not all people in any country agree on everything.

What is Dahieh?

Dahieh is a part of a very big city in Lebanon. Imagine a really big neighbourhood in one of our cities, with many houses and roads. That's a bit like Dahieh. This place is important because the group called Hezbollah often stays there. It's like their main area or a place where they have many of their offices and people. That's why the news says it's a 'stronghold' – it just means a place where they are very active. **Did you know?** Cities are amazing places! They have tall buildings, busy markets, and lots of people living and working close together. Kids in cities go to school, play with their friends, and visit parks, just like kids in smaller towns or villages.

Why Do Grown-Ups Fight Sometimes?

It's a really good question to ask why grown-ups fight. Sometimes, countries or groups of people disagree very, very strongly about land, or power, or what they believe is right. These disagreements can get so big that they become conflicts or fights. It's a bit like when you and your friend might argue over a toy, but much, much bigger and with entire countries. When people feel very angry or scared, instead of finding a way to talk and understand each other, they sometimes choose to fight. Fighting is never a happy thing. It makes people sad, scared, and can hurt many innocent people. Everyone hopes for peace.

What Does "Strikes" Mean?

When Mr. Benjamin Netanyahu said he ordered "strikes" on Dahieh, it means he told his country's army or air force to hit certain places in Dahieh. This is usually done from far away, using special planes or other equipment that can send things to hit a target. It's not people physically hitting each other with their hands. It means that machines or weapons are used to hit buildings or areas from a distance. These "strikes" are usually a response to previous "attacks." An "attack" is when one side tries to hurt the other, or damage their property. It’s part of a conflict, and it can be very scary for people living nearby.

What Does This Mean for People?

When there is fighting, especially when places like cities are involved, it means that the people living there can be in danger. They might feel very scared. Homes can be damaged, and people might have to leave their houses to find a safer place. Imagine if your school or home was suddenly not safe – it would be a very worrying time. Kids everywhere want to feel safe, go to school, play with their friends, and sleep soundly in their beds. When there is conflict, it makes it hard for people to live normal, happy lives. Everyone hopes that people can find a way to live together peacefully.

Hoping for Peace

It's really sad when countries or groups of people fight. It's much better when people can talk about their problems and find kind ways to solve them. Just like we learn to share and be kind to our friends, countries can also learn to be kind to their neighbours. As your news reporter, I want to remind you that even when we hear about big, difficult things, we can always hope for peace. We can hope that leaders and people everywhere choose to talk instead of fight. We can all hope for a world where everyone is safe and happy. Thank you for listening, my wonderful young friends! Let's always hope for kindness and understanding in our world.

title: Record-holder Milner retires after 24-year career
content: Former England international James Milner announces his retirement after a 24-year Premier League career.

originalTitle: I leave Liverpool exactly where it belongs - Slot

title: Williamson to miss World Cup qualifier against Spain
